heading: Omitted
label: 101 to 122 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 101 to 122, relating to Territory of Alaska, were omitted in view of admission of Alaska into the Union.Section 101, acts , ; , ; , ; , ; , ; , ; , ; , §§ 1, 2, ; , ; , , established a district court for the District of Alaska and provided for its judges and divisions.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 431 Stat. 322Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 269, § 235 Stat. 839Mar. 2, 1921, ch. 11041 Stat. 1203Dec. 13, 1926, ch. 6, § 144 Stat. 919July 31, 1946, ch. 704, § 160 Stat. 716June 1, 1948, ch. 36362 Stat. 283June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 962 Stat. 986Aug. 23, 1954, ch. 83668 Stat. 772Mar. 2, 1955, ch. 9, § 1(g)69 Stat. 10Aug. 1, 1955, ch. 44369 Stat. 430Section 101a was from a sentence added to R.S. § 5296 by , . R.S. § 5296 was subsequently amended in full by , , which failed to include provisions on the subject of that sentence or to refer to the 1935 amendment adding it. That sentence provided as follows:act May 24, 1935, ch. 14249 Stat. 289act June 29, 1940, ch. 499, § 454 Stat. 692“The District Court of the Territory of Alaska shall be deemed a court of the United States, and the commissioners appointed by the judges of the said District Court of the Territory of Alaska under the provisions of title I, chapter 1, section 6, of the act of (, 324 [sections 104 and 108 of this title]), shall be deemed commissioners of a United States court, within the intent and meaning of this section [former ].”June 6, 190031 Stat. 323section 641 of title 18Section 102, acts , ; , ; , ; , , provided for terms of court, special terms, and employment of interpreters.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 431 Stat. 322Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 269, § 235 Stat. 839Mar. 2, 1921, ch. 11041 Stat. 1203Nov. 22, 1943, ch. 30457 Stat. 591Section 103, , , specified the jurisdiction of the divisions of court, and provided for change of venue.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 531 Stat. 323Section 103a, , as added , , made Federal Rules of Civil Procedure applicable to district court of Territory.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 5aJuly 18, 1949, ch. 343, § 163 Stat. 445Section 104, , , authorized appointment of clerks and commissioners.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 631 Stat. 323Section 104a, , as added , , authorized appointment of Deputy Commissioners, provided for their compensation, prescribed their powers and duties, and required the posting of a bond.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 6Apr. 13, 1954, ch. 13668 Stat. 52Section 105, acts , ; , §§ 1, 39, , 926, 927, 996, required clerks and commissioners to post bonds.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1231 Stat. 326June 25, 1948, ch. 64662 Stat. 914Section 106, acts , ; , ; , ; , , detailed duties of clerk.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 731 Stat. 324Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 269, § 335 Stat. 840June 13, 1940, ch. 35054 Stat. 384June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 1062 Stat. 987Section 107, acts , ; , §§ 13, 39, , related to clerk’s fees, accounts, and clerical help.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1031 Stat. 325June 25, 1948, ch. 64662 Stat. 987Section 108, , , directed that commissioners shall be ex officio justices of the peace, empowered them to grant writs of habeas corpus, and prescribed other powers and duties.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 631 Stat. 323Section 109, acts , ; , ; , , authorized appointment of district attorneys, specified residence requirements and prescribed their duties and salaries.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 831 Stat. 324Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 269, § 435 Stat. 841June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 1162 Stat. 987Section 110, acts , §§ 9, 10, , 325; , §§ 12, 39, , 992, authorized appointment of marshals and deputies and prescribed their duties and powers.June 6, 1900, ch. 78631 Stat. 324June 25, 1948, ch. 64662 Stat. 987Section 111, acts , ; , , provided for increases in marshal’s bond.Mar. 3, 1899, ch. 429, § 45930 Stat. 1336Jan. 22, 1902, ch. 332 Stat. 2Section 112, acts , ; , , authorized appointment of attorneys, judges, and marshals.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1031 Stat. 325June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 1362 Stat. 987Section 113, acts , ; , , provided for manner of payment of salaries of judges, marshals, clerks, and district attorneys.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1031 Stat. 325June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 1362 Stat. 987Section 114, acts , ; , ; , , authorized traveling expenses.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1031 Stat. 325Apr. 6, 1914, ch. 52, § 138 Stat. 318June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 1362 Stat. 987Section 115, , , directed Attorney General to pay office expenses of United States marshals.act Jan. 3, 1923, ch. 21, title II42 Stat. 1083Section 116, acts , ; , , required preparation of accounts of fees and expenses.June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1131 Stat. 326Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 269, § 535 Stat. 841Section 116a, acts , ; , ; , , related to fees of commissioners.Mar. 15, 1948, ch. 12162 Stat. 80July 12, 1952, ch. 70166 Stat. 592Aug. 1, 1956, ch. 86470 Stat. 921Section 117, , , authorized establishment of recording districts, their modification or discontinuance, and removal of commissioner.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1331 Stat. 326Section 118, , , required keeping of record books and prescribed duties of recorders.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1431 Stat. 327Section 119, (part), , specified instruments to be recorded.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1531 Stat. 327Section 120, (part), , required accounts for fees for instruments recorded.act June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 1631 Stat. 328Section 121, , , provided for payment of costs of prosecuting crimes under Alaskan laws.act Aug. 29, 1914, ch. 29238 Stat. 710Section 122, , , exempted Territory from posting bond or undertaking in legal proceedings.act Apr. 11, 1928, ch. 35345 Stat. 422
